# Pricing

Pricing model: **base monthly fee + free order quota + per-100 overage**.

## Plans (Feb 2026 pricing)

| Plan | Base | Free orders/mo | Overage |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Starter** | $0 | 250 | n/a |
| **Essential** | $29/mo | 500 | $15/100 |
| **Advanced** | $129/mo | 2,000 | $10/100 |
| **Ultimate** | $499/mo | 7,000 | $5/100 |

**Free trials:** 14 days for Essential/Advanced, 30 days for Ultimate. Starter is free forever (no trial). The free trial can be used **only once per store** — reinstalling after uninstall does not grant a new trial. After upgrading, Joy has a **30-day refund policy** if the merchant isn't satisfied (contact support).

---

The plan quotas above are about **orders**, which is separate from **transactions**. A **transaction in Joy is not the same as a Shopify order** (1 transaction = 1 loyalty activity such as earning or redeeming points). **Transaction limits and "unlimited transactions" requests are not a pricing/plan matter** — see the unlimited-transactions case for how to handle them (collect store URL → escalate to the team; no plan check required).

---

| Feature | Starter | Essential | Advanced | Ultimate |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Referrals | 30 | 100 | Unlimited | Unlimited |
| Points expiration | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Loyalty page | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Multi-language | ❌ | Basic | Auto-translate | Auto-translate |
| Analytics | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Shopify POS | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Fraud prevention | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| VIP tiers | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Paid membership | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Rule engine | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Cart drawer redemption | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Loyalty Hub (account page) | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Milestones | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| B2B partner tiers | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Checkout extensions | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ Plus only |
| Dynamic point expiration (FIFO) | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Hydrogen / Headless | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| API access | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Shopify Flow | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Shopify Metafield sync | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Dedicated AM | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| White-glove migration | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Priority support | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |

---

Effective **February 9, 2026** for new subscribers only:

| Plan | Old | New | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Essential | $24.90/mo | $29/mo | +$4.10 |
| Advanced | $99/mo | $129/mo | +$30 |
| Ultimate | $499/mo | $499/mo | No change |

**All plans track points.** Every plan (including Free/Starter and Essential) tracks point earning, holding, and redemption — the plan changes the order quota and which advanced features are available, not whether points work.

**Legacy "Pro" plan:** "Pro" is a **legacy plan** held by merchants who installed Joy before 2023. It is no longer offered to new merchants, but legacy Pro subscribers keep it. When a feature is listed as available on "Pro, Advanced, Ultimate," that includes these grandfathered Pro merchants — it is not a renamed Advanced plan.

**Existing subscribers (subscribed before Feb 9, 2026)** keep their previous rate **permanently** — grandfathered pricing.

---

- **Cap orders?** No hard limit, but you can set alerts at 80% and 100% of quota
- **Upgrade?** Effective immediately
- **Downgrade?** Effective next billing cycle. Data stays intact; higher-plan features become unavailable
- **Annual billing?** Currently monthly only. Contact sales for annual contracts
- **Enterprise (5,000+ orders/mo)?** Custom pricing — book a demo at joy.so/book-demo
- **Agency/partner pricing?** Available — joy.so/partners

## Calculation example

3,500 orders/mo on Advanced:
- Base $129
- Free orders 2,000; overage 1,500 → 15 × $10 = $150
- **Total: $279/mo**

Calculator: joy.so/pricing.

- kb_checkout (Ultimate for checkout extensions)
- kb_settings-developers (Ultimate for API + Hydrogen)

# Widget Issues

## Cause
With Shopify Customer Accounts (new), Shopify sometimes fails to update SSO state in time after login (especially when customer goes back to a previous page), so the widget still sees them as guest.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Share this workaround with the merchant: after logging in, ask the customer to click **Join program** on the Joy widget — this re-triggers authentication and the widget refreshes.
- **Step 2:** Confirmed — this is a known Shopify SSO timing issue, not a Joy bug.
- **Step 3:** Suggest configuring Shopify to redirect customers to the previous page after login (instead of the account page). See: https://help.joy.so/how-to-redirect-customers-to-the-previous-page-instead-of-the-account-page-with-shopify-nca
- **Step 4:** Joy dev team is tracking this for a permanent fix.

## Related
- kb_widget
- kb_account-page

---

## Cause
Shopify shows this banner because Joy hasn't yet upgraded to the latest Markets and Catalogs API (2025-04). It is a default warning for any app still on an older API; it does not affect current Joy functionality.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Reassure merchant that the warning is informational and Joy still works correctly with Markets.
- **Step 2:** Tell them to keep using the app; if any actual issue arises during use, contact Support.
- **Step 3:** Note: once Joy updates to the 2025-04 API, the banner will disappear after about 7 days.

## Related
- kb_widget

---

## Collect First
Before giving instructions, ask:
- **Which widget version are they on?** Ask: *"Are you using the new Widget v4 or the classic widget? You can check by going to Joy Admin → On-site content → Widget → Setup — if you see a sidebar with Header / Body / Footer sections, that's v4. If you see tabs like Widget theme / Membership card / Display, that's v3."*
- What exactly do they want to change? (color, font, shape, size, position…)
- Why — is it overlapping another element, or just a design preference?
- Which pages and devices (desktop/mobile)?
- A screenshot of the current state is very helpful.

## Resolution Steps

**Step 1 — Guide through built-in settings first.**

Path: **Joy Admin → On-site content → Widget → Setup → Widget design**

### If on Widget v4:
The editor has a full sidebar. Guide merchant to the relevant section:
- **Colors / theme** → Settings → Design → Preset Theme or Primary/Background color
- **Layout** → Settings → Design → Layout (Widget / Drawer), Position on desktop
- **Earn & Redeem layout** → Settings → Design → Earn & Redeem layout (Tabs / Separate blocks)
- **Font** → Settings → Design → Fonts
- **Border radius** → Settings → Design → Border radius
- **Launcher button** → Settings → Launcher (style, size, alignment)
- **Widget text / labels** → Settings → Content & Translations
- **Header image or logo** → Header → Header
- **Show/hide or reorder body sections** → Body — toggle each block on/off, drag to reorder
- **Edit block content** → Body — click any block to edit text, images inline
- **Footer navigation tabs** → Footer → Footer menu
- **Hide from guests / page restriction / popup** → Advanced → Display or Popup
- **Custom CSS** → Advanced → Custom CSS

### If on Widget v3:
- **Widget theme tab** — Display type (Floating/Drawer), Color preset, Color palette (hex), Text theme, Icon theme (Minimal/Elegant/Vivid), Border radius, Float button (style, icon, label, Generate AI Icon), Button color
- **Membership card tab** — card appearance per VIP tier (text color, alignment, button color, card background)
- **Display tab** — font, language, mobile screen ratio (80%/90%/Full screen), widget content block order
- **Advanced settings tab** — Page restriction, Display after login, Instant popup, Hide widget button, Show lowest tier first, Advanced CSS

**Step 2 — If built-in settings resolve the request:** Walk merchant through the relevant section, ask them to Save and verify on storefront. Done.

**Step 3 — If built-in settings are not enough:**
- Collect: exact requirement, target look or position, which pages/devices, screenshot or hex color if applicable.
- Tell merchant: *"I'll check with our team on this — they'll follow up with you shortly."*
- Escalate to team with all collected details. Append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. Bot stops here — team handles the rest.

## Related
- kb_widget

- **Step 2:** Tell the merchant our team will configure the exclusion for them.

## Background
The default page restriction options only cover home, product, collection, cart, and blog pages. Custom page display requires a backend configuration.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** When merchant asks (or replies "Yes, I'd love to" to the auto pop-up on the Widget design page), tell them our team will enable this for them.
- **Step 2:** Collect the store URL and the specific custom page URL(s) from the merchant.
- **Step 3:** Escalate to team to enable the custom page option — append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. Bot stops here — team handles the rest.

## Related
- kb_widget


## Unified widget float button overlaps the mobile checkout button

**Symptoms:** On mobile, the Joy floating button overlaps the store's checkout / add-to-cart button, or the merchant reports the **Unified** widget loading slowly on mobile. Some merchants temporarily switch back to the **Classic** widget because of this.

**Resolution Steps:**
- **Step 1:** Reposition the launcher — **Settings → Launcher → Alignment** and adjust position/offset so it clears the checkout button.
- **Step 2:** Use page-hide rules to hide the widget on cart/checkout pages where it conflicts.
- **Step 3:** If the merchant prefers, they can switch back to **Classic** while the issue is logged.
- **Step 4:** For pixel-precise placement, collect a screenshot + device + page URL and escalate to the team to apply targeted CSS via collaborator access. Append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

# App Errors & Unexpected Behavior

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask merchant to describe the steps to reproduce: which feature they were accessing, what they clicked, and at which step the error appeared.
- **Step 2:** Ask for a screenshot of the error page.
- **Step 3:** Recommend merchant reload the page and try again.
- **Step 4:** If the issue persists → escalate to team with: store URL, steps to reproduce, and screenshot. Append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

---

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask merchant to describe the steps to reproduce: which setting or feature they were configuring, what they changed, and at which step the error appeared.
- **Step 2:** Ask for a screenshot of the error message.
- **Step 3:** Recommend merchant reload the page and try saving again.
- **Step 4:** If the issue persists → escalate to team with: store URL, steps to reproduce, and screenshot. Append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

## Storefront console error / redirect / "Missing Key" / analytics issue

**Symptoms:** Merchant reports a JavaScript console error on the storefront (e.g. on Add to cart), an unexpected redirect, a Google Analytics tracking issue, a "Missing Key" message, the app pixel showing as disconnected, or sections rendering only their titles.

**Resolution Steps:**
- **Step 1:** Collect the store URL, the exact page/action, and a screenshot of the console error.
- **Step 2:** Note these are usually theme/snippet/Liquid or configuration issues — some are merchant-side theme errors, not Joy itself.
- **Step 3:** If the app pixel is involved, check **Settings → Customer Events** to confirm Joy is enabled.
- **Step 4:** Request temporary collaborator / theme access if investigation is needed, escalate to the team, and ask the merchant to revoke access afterward. Append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

# Points Redeeming Issues

## Symptoms
- Customer redeems points -> gets a discount code -> code rejected or not applied at checkout.

## Common Causes
- Customer is also using another Shopify or third-party app discount; combination is not enabled.
- The discount code's conditions (minimum spend, eligible products, etc.) are not met.
- App bug.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask which discount codes are running on the store (automatic and manual). Check whether any run in parallel with the Joy code.
- **Step 2:** Confirm if Discount Combinations is configured to allow combining the Joy code with other codes.
- **Step 3:** Request **Discounts** access in Shopify to inspect the code's settings and conditions.
- **Step 4:** If conditions and combinations look correct, escalate to team and append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. Bot stops here — team handles the rest.

## Related
- kb_redeeming-programs
- kb_checkout

---

## Symptoms
- On dynamic discount, the redeem input defaults to 0; customer must click plus to reach the maximum allowed.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask merchant for their store URL and the specific discount code failing.
- **Step 2:** Tell the merchant our team will handle this for them.
- **Step 3:** Escalate to team to enable the max-redeem default setting — append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

## Related
- kb_redeeming-programs

---

## Symptoms
- Place Order program "Earn based on amount spent" allows decimals for store credit.
- The Point Calculator widget still displays a rounded whole number.

## Cause
- The store credit variable on the Point Calculator historically supported integers only.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask merchant for their store URL.
- **Step 2:** Tell the merchant our team will handle this for them.
- **Step 3:** Escalate to team to enable the "Display Store Credit Decimal" option — append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

## Related
- kb_earning-programs
- kb_widget

---

## Note
Store Credit is a native Shopify feature. The Joy Flow action only adds credit to customers; it cannot customize how credit displays.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Enable Store Credit in **Shopify Settings -> Customer Account**.
- **Step 2:** Verify backend: open a Shopify customer's profile, the Store Credit balance is shown and can be manually adjusted.
- **Step 3:** Verify frontend: customers see the balance in their **Profile page -> Payment methods**.
- **Step 4:** At checkout, Store Credit appears as a secondary payment method. Toggling it deducts the entire credit from the order total.

## Customer used or generated Joy discount codes that were not officially issued

**Symptoms:** A customer redeemed or generated Joy discount codes that were never officially issued, or appears to have found a loophole to create valid codes.

**Resolution Steps:**
- **Step 1:** Treat this as a security issue. Collect the customer email and the specific order / discount codes involved.
- **Step 2:** Escalate to the team so they can investigate and close the loophole at the source. Append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.
- **Step 3:** For prevention going forward, the merchant can rely on one-time-use codes (default), per-customer redemption limits, minimum-order requirements, and **Pending Points** (Advanced, Ultimate) to hold points through the return window.

- kb_integrations-shopify-flow
- kb_redeeming-programs

# VIP Tiers

Available on **Advanced, Ultimate**. Configure at **Joy Admin → Membership → VIP Tiers**.

## Tier calculation rules

| Rule | Best for |
|---|---|
| **Points earned** | Encourage broad engagement (purchases + reviews + referrals) |
| **Amount spent** | Reward high spenders |
| **Number of orders** | Reward repeat purchase frequency |

> Redeeming/spending points lowers the active points balance and can **demote** a customer on **Points earned** tiers. To keep redemption from demoting customers, use **Amount spent** (cumulative spend is not reduced by redemptions) or assign protected customers to an **Exclusive tier**.

## Per-tier points-earning rate

A VIP tier can carry a **points-earning multiplier** as a perk, so higher tiers earn points faster (for example Bronze 1pt/$1, Silver 1.5×, Gold 2×). Configure it as a tier privilege; the storefront shows the earning rate for the customer's current logged-in tier.

The alternative mechanism is a **segment-targeted earning rule** in the Rule engine (Customer segment → Bonus for VIP tier or tagged customers) when you want finer targeting.

> If the earning value shown in the popup does not match the tier setting, collect the customer email + tier + a screenshot and escalate — a display mismatch has been logged to tech before.

Discount code prefix must follow `ABCD-` format.

## Per-tier configuration

- **Tier name** — branded (e.g., "Beauty Enthusiast")
- **Threshold** — points/$/orders required
- **Entry reward** (one-time on tier reach by default; can be re-granted on re-reach — see "Re-grant entry reward after downgrade" below): Discount amount/%, Bonus points, Free product, Free shipping, BXGY
- **Privileges/perks** (ongoing while in tier) — set up under **Membership → VIP Tiers → [tier] → Tier privileges → Add perks**
- **Pre-Tier reward** — coupon when customer reaches tier threshold

**Set up a tier entry reward (exact menu path):** Joy Admin → Membership → VIP Tiers → [select the tier, e.g. Bronze] → **Entry reward** → Add reward → choose type (Free product / Discount / Bonus points / Free shipping / BXGY) → Save → **Launch** to apply.

⚠️ The entry-reward coupon (e.g. "Free socks" on reaching Bronze) is created under **Entry reward** — NOT under Tier privileges/Benefits. If a configured entry-reward coupon is "not appearing", confirm it was added under Entry reward (not Privileges) and that the tier was re-Launched after editing.

---

Starter plan merchants can fully configure VIP tiers in trial mode (tier names, thresholds, rewards, visuals) — saved configurations persist forever and activate with one click after upgrade. Cannot launch to customers and cannot activate notifications until a paid plan with VIP Tiers is active.

---

**Privileges** (perks) apply automatically on **every qualifying order** while customer holds the tier. Different from **Entry Rewards** which fire once when first reaching the tier.

**Set up a perk (exact menu path):** Joy Admin → **Membership** → **VIP Tiers** → [select the tier, e.g. Gold] → **Tier privileges** → **Add perks** → choose a privilege type (Discount / Free Shipping / Free Product / Bonus Points / Exclusive Products / Custom Reward) → configure the value → **Save** → **Launch** the tier to apply. Repeat **Add perks** to stack more than one perk in the same tier.

> Note: a one-time "reaching-the-tier" coupon (e.g. free product on Bronze) is configured under **Entry reward**, not here under Privileges/Benefits. See the vip-overview chunk for the entry-reward menu path.

| | Privileges | Entry Rewards |
|---|---|---|
| Frequency | Every qualifying order | One-time |
| Application | Automatic at checkout | Customer redeems |
| Duration | Until tier changes | Single use |

> "One-time" means once per tier-reach. If **Allow giving entry reward again after being downgraded** is enabled (Customer management), a member who drops the tier and later re-reaches it earns the entry reward again. See the "Re-grant entry reward after downgrade" section below.

## Re-grant entry reward after downgrade

By default a tier's **Entry reward** fires only once, the first time a customer reaches that tier. It does **not** fire again if they drop and re-reach the tier — unless this setting is on.

**Setting:** `Allow giving entry reward again after being downgraded` (a checkbox).
**Where:** Joy Admin → **Membership** → **VIP Tiers** → **Tiers** tab → **Tier configuration settings** → **Customer management** (just below "Customize tag prefix", next to "Enable tier point editing").

**What it does (in-app tooltip):** "When enabled, customers who were previously downgraded can receive the entry reward again after re-entering the tier. This applies to downgrades caused by admin adjustments (manual edits or tier imports), tier demotion."

So with this enabled, a member who reaches a tier, drops out of it, then reaches it again **will** receive that tier's entry reward a second time. Covers downgrades from: admin manual edits, tier imports, and natural tier demotion.

## Privilege types

Discount Amount, Discount Percentage, Free Shipping, Free Product, Bonus Points, **Exclusive Products/Collections**, Custom Reward.

Multiple privileges can stack within one tier.

## Exclusive products for VIPs

Restrict products/collections so only customers in selected tiers can purchase them. Powered by **Shopify Functions** — runs natively at checkout, no theme hacks. Set up: **Membership → VIP tier → Tier privileges → Add perks → Exclusive products/collections** → choose Collection (recommended) or Product, write **Unlock message** (use `{{tierName}}` placeholder).

Tip: combine with Shopify's **Unlisted** product status to fully hide from storefront. Customers using **Translate & Adapt** should refresh and remove restricted items if switching languages mid-cart.

---

Available on **Advanced, Ultimate**. Manually-assigned tiers separate from the auto VIP system — perfect for ambassadors, wholesale, influencers.

## Setup

1. **Membership → New tier** → check **"Exclusive tier (Manual only)"** → set entry rewards + perks → Save
2. **Joy Admin → Customers → [name] → Assign to exclusive tier**

## Limits

- Each customer in **one exclusive tier** at a time
- Manual assignment only — no auto progression
- Members in exclusive tier **don't progress** through VIP tiers concurrently

---

Available on **All plans** at **Joy Admin → Notifications**. Two layers: **global toggle** (master), then per-program toggles.

Available emails: Birthday rewards, Earn points, POS redemption, Redeem points, Referral coupons/points, Referral invite, VIP tier reach, Points expiry (coming soon).

Send via Joy default sender, custom sender, or your own SMTP.

---

Available on **Ultimate** (Shopify Plus). Requires Joy support to enable.

## How it works

3 parameters at **Membership → VIP Tier → Assessment**:
- **Assessment Cycle (X)** — review every X months
- **Order Review Timeframe (Y)** — orders within last Y months are evaluated
- **Spending Threshold (Z)** — minimum spend in Y to retain tier

Only available when tiers are based on **Amount spent** (disabled if Points-based).

## Assessment vs simple demotion

| | Assessment | Demotion |
|---|---|---|
| Trigger | Spend below threshold in window | Failed to earn points in period |
| Variables | 3 (X, Y, Z) | 1 (X) |
| Use case | Strategic Plus-tier mgmt | Simple inactivity downgrade |

# Customer Management

**Joy Admin → Customers** = the hub for after-launch ops. The grid shows:

- Name & email
- Membership type — Member / Guest / Left
- Points (active balance)
- VIP tier
- VIP point (amount contributing toward tier)
- Birthday
- Tier updated, Last updated timestamps

Search by name/email; filter by segment or tier. Click any row to open the full customer profile for point adjustments, coupon revocation, tier reassignment, and activity audit.

---

| Type | Earn | Redeem | Sees widget |
|---|---|---|---|
| Member | Yes | Yes | Full status |
| Guest | If guest earning enabled | No | General info only |
| Left program | No | No | Hidden |

## How customers become Members

- **SSO / New Customer Accounts**: customer is **always a Member** once Shopify identifies them — no separate registration needed
- **Legacy customer accounts**: customer must manually register in store
- Optional: Set all customers as Guest by default and manually approve who can join

Use **Left program** for wholesale, internal staff, test accounts, or anyone who must be excluded.

---

Quick Search bars on the **Joy homepage** and **Reward Programs page**. Search by:
- Customer email
- Order ID
- Coupon code
- Program name (on programs page)

Returns customer profile, order activities, coupon usage, redemption history.

---

## Add customer inline (Shopify Admin Intents)

In **Joy → Customers → Add customer** opens a Shopify-native modal inside Joy (uses `app.intents.invoke`). Same applies to creating/editing products and collections inside reward setup. **Requires Joy embedded mode** — not available standalone.

## Import customer data (CSV)

**Customers → Import**. Use Joy template or your own file.

**Import actions:**
- Add points to existing balances (positive adds, negative subtracts)
- Reset existing balances
- Update birthdays

**Birthday column** must be named `Birthday(MM/DD)` with format **MM/DD** (zero-padded — `03/03` correct, `3/3` wrong).

**Adjusting points does NOT change tier status** — tiers update only through VIP rules or manual tier change in customer profile.**Adjusting points does NOT change tier status** — tiers update only through VIP rules or manual tier change in customer profile.

**Import matches on email already in Shopify.** The "Non-existent customers" error means an email in the CSV does not yet exist as a Shopify customer — customers must exist in Shopify first, and the email must match exactly (no typos or extra spaces). Re-importing the same file **updates existing customers (matched by email), it does not create duplicates**, so there is no need to delete the current list first.

**Orders imported at 0 points / retro backfill.** If past orders imported without points, the team can delete and re-add points with the correct rule — confirm the date range and which customers already had manual points first, to avoid double-crediting.

**Export.** The default export may not include the **VIP tier column** — the team can enable tier in the export on request. A tier-only file can be re-imported to bulk-update tiers without touching points. A full programmatic API export is **Ultimate**.

---

Joy syncs with **Shopify Customer Segments**. Excluded customers can't earn/redeem, don't see widget, can't access tiers/referrals.

## Setup

1. **Shopify Admin → Customers → Customer segments → Create segment** (e.g., tag "B2B")
2. Joy auto-syncs the segment
3. **Joy Admin → Settings → Pre-launch → Excluded segments** → select segments

Real-time eligibility — no manual customer-by-customer management.

# Points Earning Issues

## Symptoms
- Customer placed an order but balance shows no point increase.
- Activity log shows no Place Order earning event.

## Common Causes
- Order status does not match the trigger setting (Paid vs Fulfilled).
- Place Order program is restricted by customer type (All members / VIP only / Member & non-member) and the customer doesn't qualify.
- Order paid partially or fully with Store Credit while merchant excluded Store Credit from earning (Free/Essential plans exclude the entire order, not just the credit portion).
- Bug in app.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask for order number, customer email, current order status. Request Order access if needed.
- **Step 2:** Check **Joy Admin -> App Settings -> Order** and **Place Order -> Reward setting -> Apply for which type** to confirm trigger condition.
- **Step 3:** Compare order status and customer type to the program settings to identify mismatch.
- **Step 4:** If the order was paid partially with Store Credit:
  - On Free/Essential: explain that excluding Store Credit excludes the whole order, no points awarded.
  - On Advanced/Ultimate: recommend using Rule Engine and ticking the "exclude store credit only" option there.
- **Step 5:** If settings and order data match expected behavior but points still missing, escalate to team to investigate and append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

## Escalation
- Settings match but no points awarded -> escalate to team with order ID, customer email, screenshots, and append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

- kb_earning-programs (place order rules)
- kb_rule-engine (advanced exclusion rules)
- kb_pricing (plan-based feature limits)

---

## Symptoms
- Merchant requests retroactive points for old orders.
- Often happens after install, after changing earning rules, or when merchant forgot to switch program to Live mode.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask merchant why they need the points update — is it to welcome back existing customers, or to retroactively credit points for actual orders?
- **Step 2 (Case A - encourage existing customers to come back):**
  - Suggest adding a fixed bonus (100–200 pts) to all existing customers via CSV adjustment.
  - Recommend sending a newsletter announcing the loyalty program.
  - Review their reward setup and propose engagement programs (signup, follow social, birthday, review, referral).
- **Step 3 (Case B - merchant truly needs points based on actual orders):**
  - Collect from merchant: store URL, date range for the update, and whether VIP tier should also be re-calculated.
  - Tell the merchant our team will run the past-order points update for them.
  - Escalate to team with: store URL, date range, whether VIP tier should also be recalculated. Team will run the past-order points update. Append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. Bot stops here — team handles the rest.
  - For all-time updates, the merchant can use **Settings -> Sync points for past orders** themselves.

## Escalation
- If the update doesn't reflect, escalate to team and append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

## Related
- kb_points-advanced (sync past orders, dev tools)
- kb_vip-tiers (re-calculation of tiers)

---

## Symptoms
- Program no longer behaves as expected (earning wrong, redeem not applying, milestone not triggering).
- Merchant says they never changed it or doesn't remember the previous setup.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** To audit a specific program's history, collect the merchant email, store URL, and program name (or Program ID if available).
- **Step 2:** Tell the merchant our team will investigate the program history for them.
- **Step 3:** Escalate to team with the merchant email and program name for investigation. Append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. Bot stops here — team handles the rest.

## Notes
- Supported for any program that has a Program ID: earning, redeeming, perks, exclusive deals, milestones, etc.

## Related
- kb_earning-programs
- kb_redeeming-programs

---

## Decision Points
- **Q1: How is "first order" defined?** Shopify order history, or app's own logic? This decides the whole setup.
- **Q2: Stack with other programs?** If yes, single program with stacking allowed; if no, separate first-order program with highest priority and "Stop giving rewards after this program" enabled.
- **Q3: Existing customers - should past orders be adjusted?** Customers with prior orders won't qualify as first-order; confirm with merchant.

## Resolution Steps - Case A: First Order = Shopify history
- **Step 1:** Use the built-in Shopify segment **"Customers who have purchased at least once"**.
- **Step 2:** In **Shopify Flow**, create a flow:
  - Trigger: Customer joined segment "Customers who have purchased at least once"
  - Action: Add tag (e.g. `ordered_before`)
- **Step 3:** Set up programs:
  - First Order Program: highest priority, Stop giving rewards after this program ON, condition = customer does NOT have tag.
  - Subsequent Orders Program: lower priority, condition = customer HAS tag.
- **Note:** Old customers won't be auto-tagged; merchant may need to bulk-tag manually.

## Signup / welcome bonus points not awarded

**Symptoms:** A customer signed up but the signup/welcome bonus points were not added.

**Common Causes:**
- The customer did not complete the exact signup action tied to the bonus.
- The customer is not logged into a customer account with that email.
- The program was in **Sandbox**, not live, when they signed up.

**Resolution Steps:**
- **Step 1:** Confirm the signup program is live (not Sandbox) and the customer logged in with the bonus email.
- **Step 2:** If everything checks out, add the points manually via **Customers → adjust points**.
- **Step 3:** For repeated misses, escalate with the affected customer emails.

## Resolution Steps - Case B: First Order = App logic via Shopify Flow
- **Step 1:** In Shopify Flow, trigger on `Order created`, condition `Customer does NOT have tag ordered_before`.
- **Step 2:** Actions: Adjust point/credit (Joy action) + Add tag `ordered_before`.
- **Step 3:** Inside Joy app, programs that should only apply to subsequent orders must have a condition requiring tag `ordered_before`.

## Escalation
- Confirm logic and impact with merchant before triggering. Adjust legacy customer points on request.

# Referral Program

Set up at **Joy Admin → Reward programs → Referrals**. Basic features on All plans; advanced (Shopify customer/order tagging) on Essential, Advanced, Ultimate.

## Reward types

| For | Available reward types |
|---|---|
| **Referee** (new customer) | Points, Store credit, Discount %, Discount amount |
| **Referrer** | Points, Store credit (fixed), Store credit (% of referee order), Discount %, Discount amount |

- **Tier-based rewards** — Customize referrer reward by VIP tier
- **Max amount cap on % discount** — e.g., "15% off, up to $30" — set independently per side, off by default

## Referral redirection URL

Default = homepage. Change to any landing page (e.g., a campaign page).

## Sharing channels

Customers share their unique link via the loyalty widget across selected social/email channels. Email invite (see next chunk) is built-in.

---

## Flow

1. Referrer shares their unique link
2. Referee clicks link → lands on referral page → enters email to claim → **7-day cookie** stored on device → activity = pending
3. Referee places order with the **same email** → activity = complete on thank-you page → both rewarded

## Troubleshooting (referrer didn't get reward)

- **Different emails** — referee used different email at claim vs checkout. Both sides need same email
- **App pixel disabled** — check **Shopify Admin → Settings → Customer Events → Joy** is enabled. Toggle program off/on to re-sync
- Cookie expired (>7 days between click and purchase)

## Common false negatives when testing

- **"Running in test mode" message** means the referral program is in **Sandbox / preview** — go live for real rewards to be issued.
- **Same PC / same browser:** on a device where the merchant is logged into their own store, the referral page may show only the code without prompting for a friend's email. Test in incognito, another account, or a phone to see the real behaviour.

## Anti-cheat

Built-in fraud prevention prevents customers cheating with multiple accounts. Configure limits per program in the referral settings.

---

Available on **All plans**. Referrer enters friend's email in the widget → system auto-sends a branded invitation email containing the referrer's name, the discount/reward, and the referral link.

Delivery channels supported:
- Joy's built-in email
- Klaviyo
- Shopify Flow

When the friend purchases, both sides get rewards + confirmation emails.

# Billing & Refund Handling Playbook

## Overview

Billing and refund cases are sensitive. CS must follow a strict verification + escalation process. **CS does NOT issue refunds or discounts directly.**

## Standard Flow

**Step 1: Gather info from merchant**
- Ask for invoice / charge screenshot from Shopify Admin → Settings → Billing → Bills
- Ask for the reason they're requesting a refund
- Confirm store URL
- Identify charge type: monthly fee, usage fee, upgrade activation, post-cancel charge

**Step 2: Verify & explain**
- Check current plan and charge history in Joy Admin
- For usage fee disputes: count orders and compare against plan's free-order quota
- Explain the charge logic clearly:
  - Usage fees: tracked from trial start; Shopify billing starts after 14-day trial
  - Post-cancellation charge: cycle-end charge for usage already accrued is normal
  - Upgrade activation: explain when triggered and what was unlocked

**Step 3: Escalate to Billing team**
- CS cannot approve refunds — always escalate
- Create a refund request for Billing with: store URL, charge details, amount, reason, evidence (invoice screenshot)
- **Append the escalation tag to every message in the conversation** so the Billing team has full context
- For discount/offer requests: forward to `#sales-cs-success` and tag PM — never offer discounts unilaterally
- For development store cases: forward to Sales with note "development store"

**Step 4: Follow up**
- Track ticket until Billing/Sales confirms outcome
- Communicate decision to merchant with a clear explanation
- Send cancellation/downgrade guidance to prevent recurrence

---

## Case Types

**General inquiry (plan comparison, how to upgrade):**
CS handles directly — explain plan features and guide merchant through upgrade in Shopify Admin.

**Charge confusion (why charged, unexpected amount):**
Explain the charge. Check merchant's charge history, review plan usage, provide invoice details.

**Cancellation request:**
1. Understand why they want to cancel
2. Address concerns if possible
3. If they proceed → guide through downgrade to Free plan

**Refund request — simple cancellation (subscribed by mistake):**
- Guide merchant to downgrade to Free plan first
- Collect invoice screenshot + reason
- Escalate to Billing (Step 3 above)

**Refund request — dissatisfaction or bug-related:**
- Understand what they expected vs what happened
- Offer to resolve: if setup issue → guide again; if bug → fix it
- If resolved and merchant satisfied → encourage continued use; consult CS Leader re: next-month discount
- If still wants to cancel → collect info, downgrade, escalate to Billing
- **High-risk:** if merchant won't accept explanation after clear communication → do NOT argue, escalate to CS Leader/CSM immediately

**Dispute / Chargeback:**
Immediately escalate to CS Leader with full context. Do not negotiate.

---

## Pause instead of cancel

If a merchant wants to pause rather than cancel, two options: downgrade to **Free** (config + points are retained), or **pause earning** by putting programs in **Sandbox** and turning notifications off. Both keep their data intact.

## Refund Eligibility

**Likely eligible:**
- Merchant did not use paid features after the charge
- Cancelled in good faith, charge timing was confusing
- Clear billing error or duplicate charge
- Misleading upgrade prompt

**Not eligible:**
- Features were used during the billing cycle
- Merchant requesting goodwill discount only → route to Sales
- Merchant simply changed their mind after active use

---

## Common Billing Questions

**How long does a refund take?**
7–10 business days. If over 10 days, advise merchant to check with Shopify and their bank directly.

**Charged after uninstalling/downgrading. Why?**
If they uninstalled/downgraded after the billing cycle started, they receive a full invoice for that cycle but are refunded for unused days.

**Shows Free plan but was charged. Why?**
They may have uninstalled (auto-cancels subscription), then reinstalled (defaults to Free), but the final bill from the previous cycle was still pending.

**Two charges in the same month?**
Ask for charge breakdown screenshot. If different billing periods → explain. If same period → check with CS Leader. May be caused by exceeding billing threshold before regular billing date.

**When will the merchant be charged?**
On their regular Shopify billing date, together with Shopify subscription and other app charges.

**Where to see charges?**
Shopify Admin → Settings → Billing → Bills.

**Why did payment fail?**
Common reasons: expired card, no payment method, insufficient funds, card doesn't support recurring USD payments.

---

# Subscription App Integrations

Joy works with **any subscription app using Shopify Selling Plans + Contracts** (the Shopify standard).

## Tested integrations

- **Joy Subscription** (deepest, native — auto-connects)
- **Shopify Subscription** (native)
- **Recharge** (full integration with discount sync)
- Any other selling-plans-compliant app

## Two main use cases

1. **Reward subscription orders** — points for each renewal/recurring billing cycle
2. **Redeem coupons on subscription cycles** — Joy discount codes apply on subscription orders

Make sure your subscription products and selling plans are set up first.

---

## Joy Subscription

Auto-connects. **Joy Loyalty → Integrations → Joy Subscription** — shows **Connected** tag if successful. Free Advanced upgrade for Subscription if you're on Loyalty Advanced.

## Shopify Subscriptions

Install Shopify Subscriptions app first. Then **Joy Loyalty → Integrations → Shopify Subscription → Connect → Set Up** the reward program.

Both feed into the **Place subscription** earning rule (kb_earning-programs).

---

Two functions:
1. **Discount code sync** — Joy redemption coupons auto-sync to Recharge so customers apply them to subscription orders via Recharge customer portal
2. **Subscription rewards** — points awarded for subscription purchases and renewals

Connect at **Joy Admin → Integrations → Recharge**.

## Subscription renewals from non-native apps need the Flow bridge

Shopify does not natively pass subscription **renewal** data to apps. For subscription apps that are not natively integrated (e.g. Seal, BOLD, Subi), connect **Shopify Flow** (Settings → Integrations) and import the **"Joy Loyalty - Subscription Order Data Sync.flow"** template — then renewals earn points like one-time orders. See kb_milestone and kb_integrations-shopify-flow.

# Earning Programs

All earning programs live at **Joy Admin → Reward programs → Earning programs → Add rule**.

| Program | Plans | What it rewards |
|---|---|---|
| Place order | All | Points or store credit per purchase |
| Place subscription | All | Recurring subscription orders |
| Sign up | All | Joining the loyalty program |
| Sign up newsletters | All | Subscribing to newsletter (Shopify marketing status must be "subscribed", not "pending") |
| Birthday reward | Essential, Advanced, Ultimate | Customer's birthday |
| Member anniversary | Advanced, Ultimate | Days since signup (e.g., 365 = 1 year) |
| Write review | Pro and above | Product reviews via Klaviyo, Air, Judge.me, Loox, Okendo, Tydal, Reviews.io, Stamped, Fera, Yotpo, Growave |
| Social activity | Essential, Advanced, Ultimate | Follows, likes, shares on social platforms |
| Instagram comment / story reply / story mention / live comment | Advanced, Ultimate | Specific Instagram interactions (Business or Creator account required) |
| Google reviews | Essential, Advanced, Ultimate | Reviews on Google Places — admin approves before points granted |
| Fill out a survey | All | Survey completion via Shopify Flow |
| Visit website | All | Customer visits to store |
| Streak bonus challenge | All | Consecutive-day visits with milestone bonuses |
| Custom program | Advanced and above | Custom triggers — visit specific page, custom event, Shopify Flow |
| Submit receipt | Advanced and above | Photo of purchase receipt — admin approves |
| Submit form | Advanced and above | Proof of off-platform actions (TikTok, UGC, etc.) via custom form |

---

## Reward methods

- **Per amount spent**: 1 point per $1
- **One-time at threshold**: 100 points when order ≥ $50
- **Per item quantity**: 5 points per item
- **Fixed per order**: 10 points per order

## Reward type

- **Points** — abstract units, redeemed for discount coupons
- **Store credit** — real $ value, applied at checkout. Joy prompts inline for Shopify permission on first use; one-time grant covers all programs

## Multi-currency earning rates (Advanced, Ultimate)

Toggle **Apply different rates by currency** in Reward settings. Joy reads the **presentment currency** customer paid in. Set rates proportional to FX rates so reward value stays consistent.

## Conditions ("Check if")

**Recipe — earn only on full-price items (exclude sale items):** keep sale items in a dedicated collection (e.g. "SALE"), set the Place-order rule's product condition to **exclude** that collection, and mirror the same restriction on the redeeming program's **Applicable categories** so redemption doesn't stack on discounted items.


Customer / Order / Product / Collection — combine with **All** or **Any** logic. Common use: exclude orders by tag (e.g., orders from non-Shopify platforms).

## Trust-based earning not awarded (visit / social / Google review)

For Visit website, social, Instagram, and Google review programs, the customer must be **logged in** when performing the action. **Google review** points sit in a **pending/approval queue** the merchant must approve before they are granted. **Instagram** needs an IG Business/Creator account connected and the customer's IG username linked.

## Fraud prevention

- Max earning per rule per customer
- Earning frequency limit
- Auto-revoke on cancelled/refunded orders

## Calculation notes

- Shipping and taxes are NOT included by default (toggle "Reward for additional fee" to include them)
- Calculated on order subtotal in primary currency unless multi-currency is on

---

## Birthday reward (Essential+)

- **Date format**: MM/DD/YYYY or DD/MM/YYYY
- **Delivery**: Automatic (system grants) or Manual (customer claims)
- **Timing**: Exact birthday, calendar month, or custom range (X days before/after)
- Optional: grant reward N days before exact birthday
- Customer must have a birthday on file — see kb_birthday for how to collect

## Member anniversary (Advanced+)

Reward N days after customer joined loyalty program. Common: 30 days, 6 months, 365 days. Reward types: Points, Discount %, Discount amount. Customers see/claim from their loyalty page.

---

## Generic social activity (Essential+)

Trust-based — Joy can't verify completion via social APIs. Customer is guided to the URL; most follow through.

## Instagram programs (Advanced, Ultimate)

All require **Instagram Business or Creator account** + connection in **Joy Admin → Integrations → Instagram** (one connection covers all IG programs). Customers must link their Instagram username in the widget.

| Event | Detail |
|---|---|
| Comment on Instagram | Tracks comments on selected posts |
| Story reply | Text replies only — emoji-only reactions filtered out |
| Story mention | Customer mentions your brand in their story (24h window) |
| Live comment | Comments during your IG Live |

Stories require the Instagram mobile app. Detection happens while content is live.

---

## Write review (Pro+)

Connects to your review app: Klaviyo, Air Reviews, Judge.me, Loox, Okendo, Tydal, Reviews.io, Stamped, Fera, Yotpo, Growave. See kb_integrations-reviews for per-app setup.

## Google reviews (Essential+)

- Enter your Google Places URL
- After customer leaves a review, merchant sees it in **Activities** panel
- **Earning points** = approve before granting; **Instant points** = grant immediately

## Fill out a survey (All)

Connects via **Shopify Flow**. Merchant clicks **Create Shopify flow** → flow links survey completion (e.g., Zigpoll) to point reward.

---

## Visit website (All)

Set points per visit + frequency limit (daily/weekly/monthly). Pair with **Visit Website Popup** in Branding for on-site prompt.

## Streak bonus challenge (All)

Enhanced visit program — daily points + bonus on streak completion. Drives habit-forming engagement.

## Custom program (Advanced+)

Triggers beyond defaults: **Visit a page** (URL exact or contains), **Custom events**, **Shopify Flow** triggers. Build your own logic.

## Submit receipt (Advanced+)

Customer uploads receipt photo → staff approves/rejects in admin. Use case: in-store purchases through POS. Anti-cheat: limit submissions per timeframe.

## Submit form (Advanced+)

Customizable proof form for off-platform actions (TikTok hashtag posts, UGC, external reviews). Optional **Instant reward** gives small points on submit before review.

## Important block notes

# VIP Tier Issues

## Symptoms
- Customer reached a VIP tier but the expected Privilege rewards (free shipping, discount, exclusive access, etc.) are not received or not visible.

## Common Causes
- Privilege reward not configured or misconfigured in the tier.
- Display conditions on the privilege are too restrictive.
- Privilege requires the **Advanced** plan; merchant might be on a lower plan.
- Bug.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask for the customer's email so the team can check their history.
- **Step 2:** Verify that the privilege is configured.
- **Step 3:** Confirm merchant is on Advanced or higher (privilege feature is plan-locked).
- **Step 4:** Explain how privilege rewards work: they differ from entry rewards — they appear as ongoing benefits on the account page when conditions match, not as instant codes.
- **Step 5:** If setup is correct but customer still misses the privilege, escalate to team with customer email, tier name, screenshot of privilege configuration, and append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

## Common Causes
- Entry reward not configured or misconfigured.
- Entry reward was created **after** the customer already reached the tier (entry rewards do not apply retroactively).

## Resolution Steps

- **Step 2:** Ask merchant whether the entry reward was created before or after the customer reached the tier.
  - If after: explain that entry rewards only apply to customers who reach the tier after the reward is set up. Existing tier members are not retroactively rewarded.
- **Step 3:** If reward was created before the customer reached the tier and still missing, escalate to team with: customer name/email, VIP tier, entry reward setup time, and append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

## Symptoms
- Merchant asks whether a tier **Entry reward** (bonus points, discount, free product, etc.) can be received **more than once** — specifically when a member dropped the tier and then achieved/re-reached it again.

## Answer
- **Yes, it can — if the merchant enables one setting.** By default an entry reward is one-time (fires only the first time the customer reaches the tier). But there is a setting that lets a downgraded member earn the entry reward again on re-reaching the tier.
- **Setting:** `Allow giving entry reward again after being downgraded` (checkbox).
- **Where:** **Joy Admin → Membership → VIP Tiers → Tiers tab → Tier configuration settings → Customer management** (just below "Customize tag prefix").
- **Tooltip wording:** "When enabled, customers who were previously downgraded can receive the entry reward again after re-entering the tier. This applies to downgrades caused by admin adjustments (manual edits or tier imports), tier demotion."

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Confirm the merchant wants members to re-earn the entry reward each time they re-reach a tier after dropping.
- **Step 2:** Guide them to **Membership → VIP Tiers → Tiers → Tier configuration settings → Customer management** and tick **Allow giving entry reward again after being downgraded**, then Save.
- **Step 3:** Note the scope: this covers downgrades from admin manual edits, tier imports, and tier demotion. With it **off**, the entry reward is strictly one-time per customer per tier.

## Tier perk not applying on POS, or a tier reward granted twice

**POS:** if a per-tier discount/perk applies on web but not on **Shopify POS**, run a live test logged in as a tier member through cart → checkout on both web and POS, then escalate with the exact flow, customer email, and tier.

**Reward granted twice:** if a customer received the same tier reward twice (and possibly used both), collect the customer email + order and escalate so the team can check whether others were affected and remediate. (This is distinct from the intended re-grant after a downgrade/re-upgrade.)

# Customer Account Issues

## Cause
Shopify creates two separate customer IDs when identifying info doesn't match exactly - e.g. when a customer buys in-store via POS without using the same email/phone as their online account. Joy then sees them as two distinct customers.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Verify with merchant that the two accounts are the same person; collect both emails.
- **Step 2:** Explain to merchant that Shopify creates separate IDs when contact info differs, so Joy reflects two users.
- **Step 3:** Suggest merging the two accounts. If merchant agrees, escalate to team to perform the merge and append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. After merging, Joy will sync points/tier into the single remaining account.

## Notes
- CS does not merge accounts directly in Joy - it depends on Shopify customer merge.
- Always explain how Shopify and Joy identify customers to avoid confusion.

## Customer Accounts (new)

A customer added in-store (**POS**) reaches the same loyalty account online by logging in with the **same email** — with New Customer Accounts they use the email / one-time-code flow (no password to set). In-store and online activity tie to the same email and the same points balance.

- Shopify uses single sign-on (email-only login). Anyone who enters their email at checkout or for newsletter is auto-created with status **Enabled**.
- Joy receives "Enabled" from Shopify and treats them as **Member**, even if they never created an account.
- Therefore newsletter signups and order placers are classified as Members.

## Legacy (classic) Accounts
- **Member**: customer who actively created an account with a password.
- **Guest**: customer who placed an order or signed up without creating an account.
- Joy distinguishes the two; merchant can reward signup points only to true Members.

## How to Decide
- If merchant wants to reward signup-style points only to people who really create and log in -> recommend Legacy Customer Accounts.
- Otherwise, keep New Accounts and accept that all "Enabled" customers are Members in Joy.
- Explain to merchant: with New Customer Accounts, Shopify automatically enables all customers, so Joy treats them as Members. With Legacy Accounts, only customers who actively register and log in are Members. If they want signup points only for true registrants, recommend switching to Legacy Customer Accounts.

# Collecting Customer Birthday

Available on **All plans**.

## Where birthday data lives

Joy reads from BOTH locations and keeps them synced:
- **Shopify Customer Notes** under the field name `Birthday` (or your configured name)
- **Shopify Customer Metafields** if a valid date metafield exists

## Limitations & options

- **Choose the gift:** when several birthday gift options are configured, a setting lets the customer pick one from the set.
- **POS can't capture birthdays:** Shopify POS does not capture a birthday at signup — customers add it in their Joy profile online (or via the registration form / metafields below).

## Method 1 — Registration form

1. **Joy Admin → Settings** → set **"Birthday field name in register form"** to `Birthday` (or your preferred name)
2. Edit theme `customers/register.liquid` and insert Joy's birthday field snippet (date picker)
3. On registration, value saves to Shopify customer notes → syncs to Joy via Shopify customer creation webhook

## Method 2 — Sync from metafields

If birthdays already live in **Shopify Customer Metafields** (manually maintained, migrated, or set by a third-party app), Joy reads the metafield directly. No form changes needed.

## Format

Birthdays must be valid date format. CSV imports require the column **`Birthday(MM/DD)`** with format `MM/DD` (zero-padded — `03/03` correct, `3/3` wrong).

## Why birthdays matter

- Power **Birthday reward** earning rule
- Power **Member exclusive deal** dynamic active dates ("Month of birth")
- Used by **Place order** date range option ("Month of birth")

Joy stands out by translating **checkout extensions and customer account UI** — many other loyalty apps cannot.

38 supported languages, all fully customizable — every label, message, button text editable to match brand voice.

---

## Setup

**Joy Admin → Settings → Translations** → choose default language. Advanced+ enable **Detect Language** to auto-pick from IP / browser settings.

## Editing

Pick the language → edit any label/message/button per surface. Save. Updates apply to live storefront immediately.

## Known limitations

- Translations entered on the **Classic** widget carry over when switching to **Unified** — no re-entry needed.

## Checkout limitation

Shopify Checkout UI extensions don't support i18n by IP/browser. You can only set **one default language** at the checkout level via Theme Editor. The rest of Joy (widget, loyalty page, account page) supports auto-detect normally.

# Shopify Flow Integration

Available on **Essential, Advanced, Ultimate**. Install Shopify Flow if needed.

## Setup

1. **Joy Admin → Integrations → Shopify Flow → Connect / Enable toggle**
2. Open Shopify Flow → **Create workflow** → choose **Joy Loyalty** trigger or action

## Common error

If a Joy trigger/action fires with errors: **the integration toggle isn't enabled** in Joy. Re-check **Integrations → Shopify Flow** is on.

## Triggers exposed by Joy

Tier Demotion Reminder, Point Expiration Reminder, VIP Tier Reset Reminder, 7 Days Before Birthday, Customer's Birthday, Coupons for Referrers, Customer Has Enough Points for Reward, Earn Points, Points for Referrers, POS Point Redemption.

## Actions exposed by Joy

Add points, Subtract points, Add store credit, Update tier, Send referral invite email, etc.

---

When connecting Joy Flow trigger to email apps, **no variable dropdown is shown** — type property names manually.

## Auto-issuing a coupon on order/spend conditions with a delayed start

A Flow can fire per-event to issue an exclusive coupon when conditions are met (e.g. 5 orders AND $200 spent). Note: a coupon's **validity window** controls when it can be *used*, not which orders *qualify* — qualifying by a date range is a separate Flow condition. Combining "X orders AND $Y spent AND only orders after a date" usually needs tuned Flow logic or tech-assisted setup; where Joy can't express the logic, log it as product feedback and offer the closest workaround (manually issue to a qualifying list).

## Common fields (every trigger)

| Property | Example |
|---|---|
| `Customer email` | jane@example.com |
| `Customer first name` | Jane |
| `Customer last name` | Doe |
| `Customer points` | 350 |
| `customer_id` | 6123456789 |

Each trigger adds event-specific fields (e.g., earn-points adds points earned, redeem adds coupon code).

## Klaviyo template syntax

- Multi-word names: `{{ event|lookup:'Customer email' }}`
- Single-word / underscored: `{{ event.customer_id }}`

---

Pre-built Flow templates available for: **Klaviyo, Tydal, Growave, Loox, Okendo, Reviews.io, Stamped, Yotpo, Zigpoll**, plus subscription milestone tracking.

Search Shopify Flow templates for **"Joy"** — list shows all available.

**Recommendation:** for Klaviyo, the **native Joy × Klaviyo integration** offers more trigger events and richer properties than Flow — use Flow only when you need to combine Klaviyo with extra Flow logic.

---

Store credit is **natively supported** in Joy — no Flow needed. Use Flow only for custom logic (e.g., grant credit based on order tags, customer segments, complex conditions).

## Setup (Flow path)

1. **Joy → Integrations → Shopify Flow → Turn on**
2. Click **Grant Store Credit access** to authorize Joy via Flow
3. Build workflow with Joy's "Add store credit" action

Requires Shopify new customer accounts.

# Notifications / Email Issues

## Cause
Scammers were exploiting Joy to send phishing emails. As a precaution, all stores under 7 days old that installed after 2025-01-13 have Notifications disabled. Stores older than 7 days are unaffected.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Ask merchant for the store URL.
- **Step 2:** Tell the merchant: "The Notifications feature requires a brief verification step for new stores. Our team will review and enable it for you shortly — please allow 1–2 business days."
- **Step 3:** Verify the store internally: review store content, form, signs of legitimacy. Scammers often use dev stores for phishing. Do not disclose the verification criteria to the merchant.
- **Step 4:** Forward to team with: store URL, store age, any legitimacy signals observed. Tell merchant: *"Our team will review and get back to you shortly."* Append `<escalate_human>` to the reply. Bot stops here — team handles verification and enable/reject decision.

## Customers received emails they never signed up for (incl. privacy/GDPR complaint)

**Resolution Steps:**
- **Step 1 (immediate):** turn the **global notification toggle off** to stop all Joy emails at once, then re-enable only the enrolled-member events you want.
- **Step 2:** check for a Sandbox-leak / notification-config cause (cross-ref Sandbox mode and the global notification controls).
- **Step 3 (privacy/GDPR):** escalate to the team immediately, confirm what was sent and to whom, and address the customer's data-handling concern. Treat as high-priority, not a routine deliverability ticket. Append `<escalate_human>` to the reply.

## Internal Note
The "<7 days" rule is internal logic; never disclose it to merchants.

- kb_notifications

---

## Background
Simple Custom Sender uses Avada's shared domain `send.avada.io`. SPF alignment will fail if merchant's domain has DMARC policy `reject`.

## Resolution Steps
- **Step 1:** Verify the sender email status shows **Verified**.
- **Step 2:** Check if merchant has added SPF record for **mailgun.org**. Example correct SPF:
  ```
  v=spf1 include:mailgun.org include:_spf.google.com include:_spf.protection.outlook.com include:secureserver.net ~all
  ```
  - Only one SPF record per domain. Use https://mxtoolbox.com/spf.aspx to inspect.
- **Step 3:** Check DMARC policy:
  - DMARC `reject` will block Simple Custom Sender; recommend setting policy to `none`.
  - Don't change DMARC just to bypass; only if merchant understands the impact.
- **Step 4:** Recommend Custom SMTP setup for 100% deliverability if merchant is on Advanced or Enterprise.
- **Step 5:** Send a test email from Joy: **Email Notification -> select template -> Send test email**.
  - Check Spam / Promotions; try a different recipient; use https://www.mail-tester.com/ for diagnostics.

## Common Causes
- Email filtered to Spam/Promotions/Updates.
- Domain or email filter is blocking emails from Avada.
- Invalid email address.
- Domain has strict DMARC policy.

## Resolution Steps

- **Step 2:** Ask merchant to check Spam/Promotions/Updates folders.
- **Step 3:** Suggest **Resend verification email** in Joy.
- **Step 4:** Check DMARC at https://mxtoolbox.com/SuperTool.aspx (DMARC Lookup):
  - If domain has `p=reject` or `p=quarantine`, Joy app shows a built-in warning.
  - Guide merchant to use **Custom Sending Domain** or **SMTP** for proper authentication, or use a different email on a domain without strict DMARC.
